expectations.text: The review flags sync-over-async caused by `.Result` on `customerApi.GetAsync`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 explicitly quotes `customerApi.GetAsync(request.CustomerId).Result` and calls it a sync-over-async issue.

expectations.text: The review flags leaking raw exception details through `Results.BadRequest(ex.ToString())`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 2 quotes `return Results.BadRequest(ex.ToString());` and explains that it exposes stack traces/internal details.

expectations.text: The review flags missing or incorrect cancellation propagation such as passing `default` into `CreateAsync`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 3 quotes `await service.CreateAsync(request, default);` and recommends passing a `CancellationToken`.

expectations.text: The review uses severity-tagged findings rather than a single unstructured paragraph.
expectations.evidence: The output is organized into numbered findings with explicit severities: `High` and `Medium`.

notes: Token counts were not exposed by read_agent, so timing data includes duration only.

expectations.text: The review flags early materialization of all orders before filtering.
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 states that `ToListAsync()` happens before `Where(...)`, causing all orders to be loaded before filtering.

expectations.text: The review flags over-fetching or unnecessary `Include(o => o.Items)` for this query.
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 and the residual risk section both call out loading the full item graph and note that `Include` should be used only if callers need `Items`.

expectations.text: The review flags missing `CancellationToken` propagation on async EF Core calls.
expectations.evidence: Finding 3 explicitly says repository async APIs do not support cancellation propagation and recommends adding `CancellationToken`.

expectations.text: The review mentions a consistency or transactional risk around `SaveChangesAsync()` followed by `auditWriter.WriteAsync(...)`, or explicitly states that the context is insufficient to confirm it.
expectations.evidence: Residual risks mention that `CancelAsync` saves before writing the audit record and recommends a transaction/outbox pattern if audit logging is required.

notes: Token counts were not exposed by read_agent, so timing data includes duration only.

expectations.text: The review flags per-iteration `new HttpClient()` creation.
expectations.evidence: The `HttpClient` lifetime finding explicitly says creating and disposing `HttpClient` inside the loop is an anti-pattern.

expectations.text: The review flags missing cancellation propagation to the HTTP call and/or `Task.Delay(1000)`.
expectations.evidence: One finding flags `Task.Delay(1000)` ignoring cancellation, and another flags the HTTP request not using the worker cancellation token.

expectations.text: The review flags unsafe null suppression on `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(... )!`.
expectations.evidence: A dedicated nullability finding calls out `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, decimal>>(json)!;` as unsafe suppression.

expectations.text: The review flags thread-safety or concurrency risk around updating `_cache` and raising `SyncCompleted` from the worker loop.
expectations.evidence: The output has explicit findings for `_cache` shared mutable state and inline `SyncCompleted` event invocation risks.

notes: Token counts were not exposed by read_agent, so timing data includes duration only.

expectations.text: The review flags sync-over-async caused by `.Result` on `customerApi.GetAsync`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 explicitly flags `.Result` as sync-over-async in the request pipeline.

expectations.text: The review flags leaking raw exception details through `Results.BadRequest(ex.ToString())`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 2 quotes `Results.BadRequest(ex.ToString())` and calls out internal detail leakage.

expectations.text: The review flags missing or incorrect cancellation propagation such as passing `default` into `CreateAsync`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 3 explicitly says cancellation is ignored because `default` is passed to `CreateAsync`.

expectations.text: The review uses severity-tagged findings rather than a single unstructured paragraph.
expectations.evidence: The response contains numbered findings with `High`, `Medium`, and `Low` severities.

notes: Token counts were not exposed by read_agent, so timing data includes duration only.

expectations.text: The review flags early materialization of all orders before filtering.
expectations.evidence: The review says the method now loads the entire orders table before filtering and explains that filtering moved from SQL to in-memory LINQ.

expectations.text: The review flags over-fetching or unnecessary `Include(o => o.Items)` for this query.
expectations.evidence: The review explicitly mentions eager loading of all `Order.Items` for every order.

expectations.text: The review flags missing `CancellationToken` propagation on async EF Core calls.
expectations.evidence: The output never mentions `CancellationToken` or missing cancellation propagation on `ToListAsync`/`SaveChangesAsync`.

expectations.text: The review mentions a consistency or transactional risk around `SaveChangesAsync()` followed by `auditWriter.WriteAsync(...)`, or explicitly states that the context is insufficient to confirm it.
expectations.evidence: The output is focused entirely on `GetOpenOrdersAsync` and does not mention `CancelAsync`, write-audit ordering, or context limitations.

notes: Token counts were not exposed by read_agent, so timing data includes duration only.

expectations.text: The review flags per-iteration `new HttpClient()` creation.
expectations.evidence: Finding 2 explicitly says creating and disposing a new `HttpClient` every second is incorrect.

expectations.text: The review flags missing cancellation propagation to the HTTP call and/or `Task.Delay(1000)`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 1 says both `GetStringAsync(...)` and `Task.Delay(1000)` do not receive `stoppingToken`.

expectations.text: The review flags unsafe null suppression on `JsonSerializer.Deserialize(... )!`.
expectations.evidence: Finding 3 explicitly calls out the null-forgiving operator on `JsonSerializer.Deserialize<Dictionary<string, decimal>>(json)!`.

expectations.text: The review flags thread-safety or concurrency risk around updating `_cache` and raising `SyncCompleted` from the worker loop.
expectations.evidence: Finding 4 flags `_cache` publication/thread-safety, and Finding 5 notes that exceptions thrown by a `SyncCompleted` subscriber can fault the worker loop.

notes: Token counts were not exposed by read_agent, so timing data includes duration only.
The skill improved total pass rate from 83.3% to 100% in this iteration, with the clearest gain on the EF Core review eval where the skill expanded the review beyond the obvious query regression.
Eval 1 and Eval 3 were non-discriminating in this run: both with-skill and baseline outputs satisfied all current assertions, so those evals validate minimum coverage more than unique skill value.
With-skill runs were slower and longer on average because the skill encouraged broader, more structured reviews. The added cost bought more complete issue coverage on Eval 2.

The benchmark's token column is an output-size proxy derived from `metrics.json` because `read_agent` did not expose total_tokens for these runs. Duration values are real elapsed times from the completed agents.
